header.title: Muscle Gain Guide
header.subtitle: Science-based approach to building muscle and strength

caloric.title: Caloric Surplus
caloric.desc: Your body needs extra energy to build new muscle tissue. Too little and you won't grow; too much and you'll gain unnecessary fat. Aim for 250-500 kcal above your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE).

protein.title: Protein Synthesis
protein.desc: Protein is the building block of muscle. Research shows that 1.8-2.2g of protein per kilogram of body weight maximizes muscle protein synthesis and recovery.

overload.title: Progressive Overload
overload.desc: Muscles grow in response to increasing demands. Each week, aim to increase weight, reps, or sets. Track your total volume (sets × reps × weight) to ensure consistent progression.

mealTiming.title: Meal Timing for Muscle Growth
mealTiming.subtitle: While total daily intake matters most, strategic timing can optimize performance and recovery:

preWorkoutCarbs.title: Pre-Workout Carbs
preWorkoutCarbs.desc: Quick carbs for energy (banana, rice cakes)

p1.title: Consistency beats perfection:
p1.desc: Missing one workout or meal won't kill gains, but inconsistency will

p2.title: Sleep is anabolic:
p2.desc: Aim for 7-9 hours nightly for optimal recovery and growth hormone release

p3.title: Track your volume:
p3.desc: What gets measured gets improved - log your lifts to ensure progressive overload

p4.title: Patience is required:
p4.desc: Natural muscle growth is 0.5-1kg per month for beginners, slower for advanced lifters

strength.awayFromTarget: You are {{gap}}kg away from your target weight's work capacity
strength.setTarget: Set your target weight below to see your volume goal

metrics.noData: No measurements logged yet. Start tracking to see your progress!

habits.title: Today's Hypertrophy Habits
habits.subtitle: Check off each habit as you complete it

recovery.title: Recovery Status
recovery.goodDesc: Great job on sleep and protein! Your muscles are recovering optimally.
recovery.improveDesc: Focus on sleep and hitting your protein target for better recovery.

rest.title: Muscle is built during rest:
rest.desc: Sleep and nutrition are when growth happens, not in the gym

protein.title: Protein timing matters less than total:
protein.desc: Focus on hitting your daily target, timing is secondary

track.title: Track everything:
track.desc: Weight, measurements, and volume - data reveals progress you can't see in the mirror

creatine.title: Creatine is proven:
creatine.desc: 3-5g daily increases strength and lean mass with no side effects for most people
